---
title: "Methods and Results (generated sections)"
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
# Anchor on the git root: this report is its own Quarto project.
source(file.path(rprojroot::find_root(rprojroot::is_git_root), "R", "01_setup.R"))
clean <- get_clean_consumer_data()

n_total <- nrow(clean)
n_cond  <- nlevels(clean$condition)

# ---- Fit the shared models (defined once in R/functions/analysis.R) -------
fit_main <- fit_main_model(clean)
med      <- fit_mediation_models(clean)
fit_mod  <- fit_moderation_model(clean)

tidy_main <- broom::tidy(fit_main, conf.int = TRUE)
b_comb <- tidy_main$estimate[tidy_main$term == "conditioncombined_frame"]
p_comb <- tidy_main$p.value[tidy_main$term == "conditioncombined_frame"]

# Keep the Overleaf-synced folder self-contained: copy the one shared
# bibliography into renders/manuscript/generated/ so manuscript.tex resolves it
# both locally and on Overleaf (which only syncs the renders/manuscript/ folder).
gen_dir <- project_path("renders", "manuscript", "generated")
dir.create(gen_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
file.copy(project_path("references", "references.bib"),
          file.path(gen_dir, "references.bib"), overwrite = TRUE)
```

```{=latex}
% GENERATED FROM reports/manuscript/methods_and_results.qmd — DO NOT EDIT BY HAND.
% Edit the prose in the .qmd, then: quarto render reports/manuscript
```

# Methods

We analysed a simulated consumer decision-making dataset of `r n_total`
participants, each randomly assigned to one of `r n_cond` message-framing
conditions (a plain control plus scarcity, sustainability, social-proof, and
combined frames). After viewing the framed product, participants reported
multi-item measures of price sensitivity, sustainability concern, brand trust,
and perceived value, followed by their purchase intention and a hypothetical
buy decision.

Multi-item scales were averaged into composite scores after reverse-scoring the
relevant brand-trust item, and a value of $-99$ was treated as missing. Data
were cleaned with scripted, version-controlled steps; the full codebook and
cleaning code live in the project repository. All models were estimated in R
with ordinary least squares (`lm`). Every figure and table in this paper is
generated directly from the analysis code, so the reported numbers cannot drift
from the analysis.

# Results

## Main effect

Relative to the control frame, the combined frame raised purchase intention by
`r sprintf("%.2f", b_comb)` points on the seven-point scale
($p = `r sprintf("%.3f", p_comb)`$). @fig-pi-by-condition shows the full pattern
across conditions.

```{r}
#| label: fig-pi-by-condition
#| fig-cap: "Purchase intention by condition. Red points are condition means with 95% confidence intervals."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
plot_pi_by_condition(clean)
```

## Mediation through perceived value

The frames also shifted perceived value upward, and perceived value strongly
predicted purchase intention (@fig-mediation-scatter); adding it to the outcome
model attenuated the condition coefficients, the signature of an indirect path.

```{r}
#| label: fig-mediation-scatter
#| fig-cap: "Perceived value predicts purchase intention; the frames shift perceived value upward, producing an indirect path."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
plot_mediation_scatter(clean)
```

## Moderation by price sensitivity

The combined-frame boost was attenuated among the most price-sensitive
respondents (@fig-moderation).

```{r}
#| label: fig-moderation
#| fig-cap: "Condition $\\times$ price sensitivity: the combined-frame boost shrinks for high-price-sensitive respondents."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
plot_moderation(clean)
```

## Model estimates

@tbl-models collects the coefficients behind the figures above. It is generated
by `reports/manuscript/methods_and_results.qmd` straight from the fitted models,
so the paper's numbers track the analysis exactly.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-models
#| tbl-cap: "Model estimates with 95% confidence intervals."
dplyr::bind_rows(
  total       = broom::tidy(med$total, conf.int = TRUE),
  mediator    = broom::tidy(med$a,     conf.int = TRUE),
  outcome_w_m = broom::tidy(med$b,     conf.int = TRUE),
  moderation  = broom::tidy(fit_mod,   conf.int = TRUE),
  .id = "model"
) |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    Model = model, Term = term,
    Estimate = round(estimate, 2), SE = round(std.error, 2),
    `CI low` = round(conf.low, 2), `CI high` = round(conf.high, 2),
    p = round(p.value, 3)
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(booktabs = TRUE)
```
